title: Bridge Essay: Conflict monitoring or multi-tasking? Tracking within-task performance in single-item and multi-item Stroop tasks.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.

**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Conflict monitoring or multi-tasking? Tracking within-task performance in single-item and multi-item Stroop tasks.** — Acta Psychol (Amst) (2022). DOI: 10.1016/j.actpsy.2022.103583. PMID: 35381473. Cognitive control is applied in situations that require overriding a habitual and automatic response. The conflict monitoring hypothesis and the Expected Value of Control (EVC) theory as its extension posit a control system responsible for detecting conflicting occasions and adapting to them dynamically within a task.
